page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton processors utilizing the 64-bit ARM64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz with no hyperthreading, providing a direct one-to-one mapping of physical cores to virtual CPUs. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates balanced performance across multi-threaded operations. The server achieves average-tier results in multi-core CPU stress tests, Passmark database operations, and Redis SET operations, which reached 38.98 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with weak ratings in Geekbench single-core and text processing, and poor performance in single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is strong for cached small blocks at 1894.04 GB/sec, but drops to 150.22 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64-based Graviton architecture delivers a dense memory-to-core ratio and high-speed local NVMe storage without the premium associated with traditional x86 architectures. This makes the r6gd.8xlarge highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from low-latency local storage, such as relational and non-relational databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing pipelines. It is less suited for single-threaded applications or workloads requiring advanced x86 vector extensions.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores on the ARM64 architecture. Operating at 2.5 GHz, it features 256.0 GB of RAM, 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show average multi-core performance, with Redis SET operations reaching 38.98 million operations per second and solid multi-threaded decompression. However, single-core performance presents a tradeoff, scoring in the lower percentiles for single-threaded tasks and text processing. The high memory-to-core ratio and integrated high-speed local storage make this instance highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.8xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 server featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB of RAM, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server with 32 dedicated cores, 256 GB RAM, and 1900 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized server featuring dedicated physical cores and high-capacity local NVMe storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 Graviton physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
256 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
1900 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing
Tradeoff of lower single-core performance compared to multi-core efficiency
